overview: The Village of Weston Tourism Commission approved four grant requests totaling $22,500 for local events including the Mountain Bay Cup soccer tournament ($5,000), GG's 5K Glow Fun Run ($2,500), Hmong Wausau Festival ($10,000), and Wisconsin Valley Fair ($5,000). The commission also scheduled a special meeting for May 18th to consider a late application from Taste and Glow Balloon Festival that was missed due to administrative error.

agenda.item: Call to order and Pledge of Allegiance
Approval of minutes from previous meeting
Grant request - GG's 5K Glow Fun Run Walk
Post event report - High School Bowling
Remarks from staff regarding Taste and Glow application

discussions.body: Tim White, Visit Wausau Executive Director, reported on upcoming events including the Wisconsin Bike Fed summit and Marathon County Parks film festival. He highlighted the World Horseshoe Tournament coming in July, noting registrations are ahead of last year's Salt Lake City event (already approaching 700 vs. 644 total last year), expecting 800+ participants. White addressed the public comment about hotel capacity, stating there are about 2,400 rooms in the greater Wausau area and 1,800 in the immediate area. He introduced new Director of Operations Jamie Rice Hecondorf and acknowledged missing the Taste and Glow application, taking responsibility for the error.

Dan Kubat, MC United Soccer Club tournament director, requested $5,000 for the May 1-3 spring soccer tournament. He reported 170 teams registered with 68.5% (116 teams) traveling from more than 90 miles away, expecting 8-10,000 people and economic impact of $700,000 to $1 million. Games will be held at Peoples, Airport Park, and Greanette Turner Center. This is a new funding request for the spring tournament; the fall tournament previously received $3,500. Renee moved to approve $5,000, seconded by Jackson. Motion passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Grant Request - GG's 5K Glow Fun Run Walk
discussions.body: Erica (online) presented the sixth annual event at Weston YMCA, expecting 600-700 participants with many from out of state including Minnesota, Texas, and Michigan. New this year is a cornhole tournament. The event secured WAOW advertising and features their signature foam machine. Renee moved to approve $2,500, seconded by Jackson. Motion passed unanimously.

Elang Jang, festival chair since 2017, requested $10,000 for the August 1-2 event. Last year drew 12,000-13,000 attendees; this year expects 14,000-15,000, making it the largest Hmong festival in Wisconsin. New this year is an under-18 volleyball and soccer tournament expected to bring 500-800 additional athletes plus families. Jang noted hotels within an hour fill completely and emphasized using local vendors. Husain moved to approve $10,000, seconded by Renee. Motion passed unanimously.

No representative present. Commission discussed that last year's request was $10,000 but only $5,000 was approved. Commissioners questioned the benefit to Weston, noting the application showed only 3-4 hotel rooms and listed hotels in Wausau and Rothschild, none in Weston. Tim White suggested the main benefit would be marketing exposure through logo placement. Husain moved to approve $5,000, seconded by Renee. Motion passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Taste and Glow Balloon Festival Application
discussions.body: Staff explained Tim White missed forwarding the Taste and Glow application. Since the event is the second weekend of July before the July 20th regular meeting, the commission scheduled a special meeting for May 18th at 4:00 PM to consider the application so they can be included in advertising materials.

publicComment: Jim Pensson of 5200 Street, Weston, commented online questioning how applicants claim up to 2,000 hotel room bookings when Weston lacks that capacity. He also asked about follow-through on after-action report commitments to coordinate with Weston restaurants and businesses, noting the application form doesn't specify how many rooms are actually in Weston.
actionItems: Approved $5,000 grant to MC United Soccer Club for Mountain Bay Cup (May 1-3)
Approved $2,500 grant to GG's Playhouse Wausau for 5K Glow Fun Run Walk
Approved $10,000 grant to Hmong American Center for Hmong Wausau Festival (August 1-2)
Approved $5,000 grant to Marathon County Agricultural Society for Wisconsin Valley Fair
Special meeting scheduled for May 18, 2026 at 4:00 PM to consider Taste and Glow Balloon Festival application
Tim White to provide report on hotel room capacity estimates for the area
title: Tourism Commission - 4/21/2026
